SWAP001-05.Offering ID:
An ENERGY STAR®-qualified refrigerator.
  • ENERGY STAR (Version 5.1) models are at least 10% more efficient than the federal standard.
  • ENERGY STAR Most Efficient (2023) models must use less than 637 kWh per year for offering with top freezer or be at least 27% more efficient than the federal standard for offerings without top freezer.
  • Advanced Tier freezers (ENERGY STAR +5%) are at least 5% more efficient than a correspondent ENERGY STAR model.
SWAP001-05.Base Case Description: The base case is a federal code compliant refrigerator or refrigerator-freezer.
SWAP001-05.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
Ineligible products for ENERGY STAR refrigerators (10% above federal standard) include:
  • Keg/beverage centers and wine coolers/chillers.
  • Refrigerators larger than 7.75 cu.ft. and 36 inches or more in height do not qualify for a compact refrigerator rebate.
Ineligible products for ENERGY STAR Most Efficient refrigerators (27% above federal standard) include:
  • Sub-compact refrigerators, all-refrigerators, and freezers.
  • Keg/beverage centers and wine coolers/chillers.
Ineligible products for ENERGY STAR +5% include:
  • Sub-compact freezers.
SWAP001-05.Quality Assurance: Verify the refrigerator size, type, and ENERGY STAR tiers.

SWAP003-04.Offering ID:
A residential ENERGY STAR® certified natural gas, electric, or heat pump (HP) clothes dryer.
  • Basic tier is based upon the ENERGY STAR specification for clothes dryers (Version 1.0).
  • Advanced tier (ENERGY STAR Most Efficient, ESME) is based upon the ENERGY STAR Most Efficient criteria. For electric heat pump clothes dryers, the CEF is based on lowest efficiency equipment on the 2019 ENERGY STAR Most Efficient list.
SWAP003-04.Base Case Description: The base case is a federal code compliant clothes dryer.
SWAP003-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /: Eligible clothes dryers must comply with the ENERGY STAR (Basic Tier) and ENERGY STAR Most Efficient (Advanced Tier) classification. Advanced Tier electric clothes dryers must be heat pump clothes dryers.
SWAP003-04.Quality Assurance: Verify that the clothes dryer meets the applicable ENERGY STAR tiers.

SWAP003-05.Offering ID:
A residential ENERGY STAR® certified natural gas, electric, or heat pump (HP) clothes dryer.
  • Basic tier is based upon the ENERGY STAR specification for clothes dryers (Version 1.1).
  • Advanced tier (ENERGY STAR Most Efficient, ESME) is based upon the ENERGY STAR Most Efficient criteria. For electric heat pump clothes dryers, the CEF is based on lowest efficiency equipment on the 2025 ENERGY STAR Most Efficient list.
SWAP003-05.Base Case Description: The base case is a federal code compliant clothes dryer, based on the fuel type.
SWAP003-05.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
Eligible clothes dryers must comply with the ENERGY STAR (Basic Tier) and ENERGY STAR Most Efficient (Advanced Tier) classification. Advanced Tier electric clothes dryers must be heat pump clothes dryers.
The replacement equipment must be “like for like” (e.g., electric to electric). Fuel substitution is not eligible.
SWAP003-05.Quality Assurance: Verify that the clothes dryer meets the applicable ENERGY STAR tiers.

SWAP004-03.Offering ID: A clothes washer that meets the minimum integrated modified energy factor (IMEF) / modified energy factor (MEF) and the maximum integrated water factor (IWF) requirement of the ENERGY STAR® Program Requirements Product Specification for Clothes Washers Version 8.0 or the 2018 ENERGY STAR Most Efficient Recognition Criteria: Clothes Washers.
SWAP004-03.Base Case Description: The base case is a top-loading or front-loading residential, or commercial clothes washer respective to the measure case that meets the federal and California Appliance Efficiency Regulations (Title 20).
SWAP004-03.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
Eligible product must meet the minimum criteria of ENERGY STAR Program Requirements Product Specification for Clothes Washers Version 8.0 or the 2018 ENERGY STAR Most Efficient Recognition Criteria: Clothes WashersENERGY STAR Residential Clothes Washer >2.5 cu.ft.
  • Volume must be >2.5 and ≤6.0 cu.ft.
  • IMEF ≥2.06 and IWF ≤4.3 for top-loading
  • IMEF ≥2.76 and an IWF ≤3.2 for front-loading
  • Machines that satisfy the ENERGY STAR Connected criteria are allowed an IMEF 5% lower than the minimum qualifying standard.
ENERGY STAR Most Efficient Residential Clothes Washer >2.5 cubic feet
  • Volume must be >2.5 and ≤6.0 cu.ft..
  • IMEF ≥2.92 and an IWF ≤3.2.
ENERGY STAR Commercial Clothes Washer
  • Volume must be >1.6 and <8.0 cu.ft..
  • MEF J2 ≥2.2 and IWF ≤4.0.
  • Leased washer must have a minimum term of 5 years and term cannot end prior to 5 years after the qualified measure is installed.

The following products are not eligible:
  • Configurations other than a front-loading or top-loading design
  • Combination washer-dryers and residential clothes washers with a heated drying functionality
SWAP004-03.Quality Assurance: Verify that the unit meets the required energy and water efficiencies.
SWAP004-03.Important Notes: The residential energy savings came from DEER. The commercial energy savings were calculated and involved weighted averages of gas and electric fuel clothes dryer and water heater, as well as of front and top loadings.

SWAP004-04.Offering ID: A clothes washer that meets the minimum integrated modified energy factor (IMEF) / modified energy factor (MEF) and the maximum integrated water factor (IWF) requirement of the ENERGY STAR® Program Requirements Product Specification for Clothes Washers Version 8.0 or the 2018 ENERGY STAR Most Efficient Recognition Criteria: Clothes Washers.
SWAP004-04.Base Case Description: The base case is a top-loading or front-loading residential, or commercial clothes washer respective to the measure case that meets the federal and California Appliance Efficiency Regulations (Title 20)
SWAP004-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
Eligible product must meet the minimum criteria of ENERGY STAR® Program Requirements Product Specification for Clothes Washers Version 8.0 or the 2018 ENERGY STAR Most Efficient Recognition Criteria: Clothes WashersENERGY STAR Residential Clothes Washer >2.5 cubic feet
  • Volume must be >2.5 and ≤6.0 cu.ft.
  • IMEF ≥2.06 and IWF ≤4.3 for top-loading
  • IMEF ≥2.76 and an IWF ≤3.2 for front-loading
  • Machines that satisfy the ENERGY STAR Connected criteria are allowed an IMEF 5% lower than the minimum qualifying standard.
ENERGY STAR Most Efficient Residential Clothes Washer >2.5 cubic feet
  • Volume must be >2.5 and ≤6.0 cu.ft..
  • IMEF ≥2.92 and an IWF ≤3.2.
ENERGY STAR Commercial Clothes Washer
  • Volume must be >1.6 and <8.0 cu.ft..
  • MEF J2 ≥2.2 and IWF ≤4.0.
  • Lease washer must have a minimum term of 5 years and term cannot end prior to 5 years after the qualified measure is installed.

SWAP005-03.Offering ID: An ozone generator installed on an existing or new commercial laundry clothes washing machine.
SWAP005-03.Base Case Description: A conventional washing machine without an ozone generator, with a hot water boiler that meets the minimum regulated thermal efficiency standard of 80% as required by the California Appliance Efficiency Regulations (Title 20).
SWAP005-03.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The washing capacity of each washing machine must be rated at ≤200 lbs.
The customer must have a natural gas-fired boiler or natural gas water heater that supplies hot water to the on-premise laundry (OPL) equipment.
The ozone laundry system(s) must be a new purchased product and installed with a new or existing commercial washing machine(s).
The ozone laundry system(s) must transfer ozone into the water with either the venturi injection or bubble diffusion process.
Laundry systems equipped with tunnel washers are not eligible.
The nonresidential ozone laundry measure is not applicable in residential or multifamily facilities and coin operated laundromats.
SWAP005-03.Quality Assurance:
Verify that the existing hot water is supplied by a natural gas boiler.
The ozone laundry system must transfer ozone into the water through venturi injection or bubble diffusion.

SWAP005-04.Offering ID: An ozone generator installed on an existing or new commercial laundry clothes washing machine.
SWAP005-04.Base Case Description: A conventional washing machine without an ozone generator, with a hot water boiler that meets the minimum regulated thermal efficiency standards as required by the California Appliance Efficiency Regulations (Title 20).
SWAP005-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The washing capacity of each washing machine must be rated at ≤200 lbs.
The customer must have a natural gas-fired boiler or natural gas water heater that supplies hot water to the on-premise laundry (OPL) equipment.
The ozone laundry system(s) must be a new purchased product and installed with a new or existing commercial washing machine(s).
The ozone laundry system(s) must transfer ozone into the water with either the venturi injection or bubble diffusion process.
Laundry systems equipped with tunnel washers are not eligible.
The nonresidential ozone laundry measure is not applicable in residential or multifamily facilities and coin operated laundromats.
SWAP005-04.Quality Assurance:
Verify that existing hot water is supplied by a natural gas boiler.
The ozone laundry system must transfer ozone into the water through venturi injection or bubble diffusion.

SWAP006-04.Offering ID: A high-efficiency dishwasher meeting the annual energy use (EAEU) ≤240-kWh and water consumption ≤3.2 gallon/cycle tier. The tier specification includes the most efficient dishwasher models available in the U.S that meet the ENERGY STAR® Program Requirements for Residential Dishwashers Version 7.0.
SWAP006-04.Base Case Description: Due to its near 100% market penetration, the base case is an ENERGY STAR version 6.0 eligible residential dishwasher with an EAEU of 270 kWh/year.
SWAP006-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The dishwasher must meet ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Residential Dishwashers (Version 7.0).
Compact residential dishwashers and commercial dishwashers are not eligible.
SWAP006-04.Quality Assurance: Verify that the unit is ENERGY STAR qualified and meets the efficiency requirement in the Offering ID table.

SWAP006-05.Offering ID: A high-efficiency dishwasher meeting the annual energy use (EAEU) ≤240-kWh and water consumption ≤3.2 gallon/cycle tier. The tier specification includes the most efficient dishwasher models available in the U.S that meet the ENERGY STAR® Program Requirements for Residential Dishwashers Version 7.0.
SWAP006-05.Base Case Description: Due to its near 100% market penetration, the base case is an ENERGY STAR version 6.0 eligible residential dishwasher with an EAEU of 270 kWh/year.
SWAP006-05.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The dishwasher must meet ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Residential Dishwashers (Version 7.0).
Compact residential dishwashers and commercial dishwashers are not eligible.
SWAP006-05.Quality Assurance: Verify that the unit is ENERGY STAR qualified and meets the efficiency requirement in the Offering ID table.

SWAP007-03.Offering ID:
The measure case specification is defined by two efficiency tiers based upon the ENERGY STAR® Program Requirements Product Specification for Room Air Conditioners, Version 5.0.
  • ENERGY STAR Basic Tier: The unit efficiency meets the ENERGY STAR v5.0 eligibility criteria.
  • ENERGY STAR Connected Tier:The connected model offers advanced controls and increased functionality, such as the ability to turn off the unit remotely without using the manufacturer specific operating system when the user is not at home and provides feedback on the energy use of the product.
As room heat pumps (HPs) are considered room air conditioners (ACs) with reverse cycle under federal and state code and ENERGY STAR, room HPs are considered as part of this measure.
SWAP007-03.Base Case Description: A room air conditioner unit that complies with the minimum specification of the California Appliance Efficiency Regulations (Title 20).
SWAP007-03.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The room AC or HP must be qualified under ENERGY STAR v5.0 or greater.
ENERGY STAR Connected Tier offerings must be noted as “Connected Capable” in ENERGY STAR.
The California Air Resources Board (CARB) requires refrigerants to have a 100-year global warming potential (GWP) less than 750. Starting from 2025, the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) requires a 100-year GWP less than 700 for all newly manufactured product; however, before 2028, higher GWP products can still be purchased.
The following products are not eligible:
  • Packaged terminal air conditioners (PTACs)
  • Portable air conditioners, room HP models with electric resistance heat as the primary heat source, and dehumidifiers.
SWAP007-03.Quality Assurance: Verify that the new unit is ENERGY STAR qualified.

SWAP007-04.Offering ID:
The measure case specification is defined by two efficiency tiers based upon the ENERGY STAR® Program Requirements Product Specification for Room Air Conditioners, Version 5.0.
  • ENERGY STAR Basic Tier: The unit efficiency meets the ENERGY STAR v5.0 eligibility criteria.
  • ENERGY STAR Connected Tier:The connected model offers advanced controls and increased functionality, such as the ability to turn off the unit remotely without using the manufacturer specific operating system when the user is not at home and provides feedback on the energy use of the product.
As room heat pumps (HPs) are considered room air conditioners (ACs) with reverse cycle under federal and state code and ENERGY STAR, room HPs are considered as part of this measure.
SWAP007-04.Base Case Description: Per the ENERGY STAR's 2022 penetration report, the base case is an ENERGY STAR v4.2 qualified room air conditioner unit. This standard practice baseline exceeds federal and state code requirements.
SWAP007-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The room AC or HP must be qualified under ENERGY STAR v5.0 or greater.
ENERGY STAR Connected Tier offerings must be noted as “Connected Capable” in ENERGY STAR.
The California Air Resources Board (CARB) requires refrigerants to have a 100-year global warming potential (GWP) less than 750. Starting from 2025, the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) requires a 100-year GWP less than 700 for all newly manufactured product; however, before 2028, higher GWP products can still be purchased.
Packaged terminal air conditioners (PTACs), portable air conditioners, room HP models with electric resistance heat as the primary heat source, and dehumidifiers are not eligible.
SWAP007-04.Quality Assurance: Verify that the new unit is ENERGY STAR qualified.

SWAP008-03.Offering ID: An air cleaner that exceeds the minimum clean air delivery rate (CADR) to watt ratio by 30% or 50%, per ENERGY STAR® Product Specification for Room Air Cleaners Version 2.0.
SWAP008-03.Base Case Description: Base case specification was based on “2016 PG&E Retail Products Platform (RPP) - Air Cleaner Laboratory Research Results” report, which exceeds federal code requirements.
SWAP008-03.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
Eligible products must meet the efficiency (CADR/W) requirements specified in the in the Offering ID table and be on the ENERGY STAR Qualified Product List (QPL).
The product must produce a minimum smoke CADR of 30.

SWAP008-04.Offering ID: An air cleaner that exceeds the minimum clean air delivery rate (CADR) to watt ratio specification by 30% or 50%, per ENERGY STAR® Room Air Cleaners: Eligibility Criteria Version 2.0.
SWAP008-04.Base Case Description: Base case specification was based on 2026 federal efficiency standards, which mirror the current minimum efficiency requirements for ENERGY STAR Room Air Cleaners v2.0.
SWAP008-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /: Eligible products must meet the efficiency (CADR/W) requirements specified in the in the Offering ID table and be on the ENERGY STAR Qualified Product List (QPL).

SWAP011-04.Offering ID: An occupancy sensor-based control that is installed on existing vending machine or beverage merchandise cooler
SWAP011-04.Base Case Description: An existing vending machine or beverage merchandise cooler with no control.
SWAP011-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
Existing beverage merchandise coolers must:
  • Have either glass sliding doors or glass pull-open doors.
  • Have a self-contained condensing unit.
  • Be used to maintain temperatures for nonperishable products.
Existing cold vending machines must contain only nonperishable bottles and canned beverages.
The beverage merchandise controller (BMC) and vending machine controller (VMC) must:
  • Turn off the lights and the compressor, as applicable, when the surrounding area is unoccupied for 15 minutes to one hour, or if there is no sales traffic for a given period of time.
  • Periodically power the cooler back on at a maximum increment of every four hours to reasonably maintain product temperatures.
The following are not eligible:
  • Host equipment with integrated low power mode capabilities.
  • Since energy savings for these measures are calculated based on energy code assumed to be in effect at the time of host equipment manufacturing date (January 1, 2016), the savings are not representative of either beverage merchandise coolers or vending machines that were manufactured under a different and more efficient energy code for maximum daily energy consumption. Therefore, beverage merchandise coolers that were manufactured on or after March 27, 2017, which must be compliant with Title 20 Chapter 4 Article 4 §1605.1 (a) Part 2 Table A-4, are not eligible. Similarly, canned vending machines that were manufactured on or after January 8, 2019, which must be compliant with Title 20 Chapter 4 Article 4 §1605.1 (a) Part 6 Table A-14, are not eligible.
SWAP011-04.Quality Assurance:
Verify that existing vending machine is not controlled.
Verify that newly installed occupancy sensors are correctly calibrated.

SWAP011-05.Offering ID: An occupancy sensor-based control that is installed on existing vending machine or beverage merchandise cooler
SWAP011-05.Base Case Description: An existing vending machine or beverage merchandise cooler with no control.
SWAP011-05.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
Existing beverage merchandise coolers must:
  • Have either glass sliding doors or glass pull-open doors.
  • Have a self-contained condensing unit.
  • Be used to maintain temperatures for nonperishable products.
Existing cold vending machines must contain only nonperishable bottles and canned beverages.
The beverage merchandise controller (BMC) and vending machine controller (VMC) must:
  • Turn off the lights and the compressor, as applicable, when the surrounding area is unoccupied for 15 minutes to one hour, or if there is no sales traffic for a given period of time.
  • Periodically power the cooler back on at a maximum increment of every four hours to reasonably maintain product temperatures.

The following are not eligible:
  • Host equipment with integrated low power mode capabilities.
  • Since energy savings for these measures are calculated based on energy code assumed to be in effect at the time of host equipment manufacturing date (January 1, 2016), the savings are not representative of either beverage merchandise coolers or vending machines that were manufactured under a different and more efficient energy code for maximum daily energy consumption. Therefore, beverage merchandise coolers that were manufactured on or after March 27, 2017, which must be compliant with Title 20 Chapter 4 Article 4 §1605.1 (a) Part 2 Table A-4, are not eligible. Similarly, canned vending machines that were manufactured on or after January 8, 2019, which must be compliant with Title 20 Chapter 4 Article 4 §1605.1 (a) Part 6 Table A-14, are not eligible.
SWAP011-05.Quality Assurance:
Verify that existing vending machine is not controlled.
Verify that newly installed occupancy sensors are correctly calibrated.

SWAP012-02.Offering ID: A modulating valve replacing a non-modulating valve of a natural gas dryer, in a commercial or residential multifamily sector, with appropriate temperature probes installed in the exhaust stack.
SWAP012-02.Base Case Description: An unmodified natural gas dryer with a single stage valve without the required temperature sensors.
SWAP012-02.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
A professional installer will be required for all installations of this product as the inlet natural gas line will have to be removed and reattached during installation.
The natural gas dryer must not be modified by any technology that would reduce the natural gas consumption beyond the manufacturer specifications.
The dryer must have an accessible gas valve assembly and room to install the modulating device in the unit and on the unit exhaust.
This measure is not eligible for dryers with a capacity of >200 lbs or <20 lbs.
A dryer that makes use of a common or dedicated steam system is not eligible.

SWAP012-03.Offering ID: A modulating valve replacing a non-modulating valve of a natural gas dryer, in a commercial or residential multifamily sector, with appropriate temperature probes installed in the exhaust stack.
SWAP012-03.Base Case Description: An unmodified natural gas dryer with a single stage valve without the required temperature sensors.
SWAP012-03.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
All devices that can reduce the energy consumed by modulating the throughput of natural gas and monitor moisture/temperature of the unit qualify for this measure. A professional installer will be required for all installations of this product as the inlet natural gas line will have to be removed and reattached during installation.
The natural gas dryer must not be modified by any technology that would reduce the natural gas consumption beyond the manufacturer specifications.
The dryer must have an accessible gas valve assembly and room to install the modulating device in the unit and on the unit exhaust.
This measure is not eligible for dryers with a capacity of >200 lbs or <20 lbs.
A dryer that makes use of a common or dedicated steam system is not eligible.

SWAP013-05.Offering ID: A high efficiency electric range with an induction cooktop, a standalone induction cooktop, a fully electric resistance cooking range, or an electric resistance wall oven replacing existing natural gas equipment. The assumptions for measure case efficiencies were informed by the Codes and Standards Enhancement (CASE) Initiative for Plug Load and Lighting Modeling (Measure Number: 2016-RES-ACM-D) and the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE), Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy Office draft life cycle cost analysis.
SWAP013-05.Base Case Description: A natural gas range, a natural gas wall oven, or a natural gas standalone cooktop. The assumptions for base case efficiencies were informed by CASE Plug Load and Lighting Modeling (Measure Number: 2016-RES-ACM-D).
SWAP013-05.Other: Program Exclusion / /: The installed equipment must be a fully electric equipment. Induction range or cooktop equipment must have either four or five burners.
SWAP013-05.Important Notes:
This is a fuel substitution measure.
HVAC interactive effects were excluded due to reduced kitchen hood consumption.

SWAP013-06.Offering ID: An electric range with an induction cooktop, a standalone induction cooktop, a fully electric resistance cooking range, or an electric resistance wall oven that meets ENERGY STAR® Program Requirements Product Specification for Residential Electric Cooking Products Version 1.0 requirement, replacing existing natural gas equipment.
SWAP013-06.Base Case Description: A natural gas range, a natural gas wall oven, or a natural gas standalone cooktop.
SWAP013-06.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The installed equipment must be an ENERGY STAR qualified electric cooking range with induction or electric resistance cooktop, ENERGY STAR qualified induction or electric resistance cooktop, or an electric wall oven.
The new electric range or cooktop equipment must have either four or five burners.
SWAP013-06.Important Notes:
This is a fuel substitution measure.
The analysis references information derived from the California Residential Saturation Study (RASS) 2019, the Department of Energy (DOE) Energy Conservation Program: Energy Conservation Standards for Consumer Conventional Cooking Products proposed rule, U.S. Energy Information Administration's (EIA) Residential Energy Consumption Survey (RECS), and ENERGY STAR Residential Electric Cooking Products Specification.
HVAC interactive effects were excluded due to reduced kitchen hood consumption.

SWAP014-03.Offering ID: The measure case is a heat pump (HP) clothes dryer that meets the minimum combined energy factor (CEF) specification of the ENERGY STAR® Program Requirements Product Specification for Clothes Dryers Version 1.1, replacing natural gas equipment. The minimum CEF was set based on the lowest efficiency equipment on the 2022 ENERGY STAR Most Efficient list.
SWAP014-03.Base Case Description: A natural gas clothes dryer that meets the dryer minimum combined energy factor (CEF) specified by the federal and state standards.
SWAP014-03.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The installed equipment must be an ENERGY STAR - qualified heat pump clothes dryer that meets or exceeds Version 1.1 minimum efficiency CEF Specification. The product does not need to be qualified as ENERGY STAR Most Efficient.
Existing base equipment must be a stand-alone gas dryer.
SWAP014-03.Quality Assurance: Verify that the new unit is ENERGY STAR qualified.
SWAP014-03.Important Notes: This is a fuel substitution measure.

SWAP014-04.Offering ID: The measure case is a heat pump (HP) clothes dryer that meets the minimum combined energy factor (CEF) specification of the ENERGY STAR® Program Requirements Product Specification for Clothes Dryers Version 1.1, replacing natural gas equipment. The minimum CEF was set based on eligibility of all the equipment on the 2025 ENERGY STAR Most Efficient list, while also exceeding CA Title 20 and federal requirement.
SWAP014-04.Base Case Description: A natural gas clothes dryer that meets the dryer minimum combined energy factor (CEF) specified by the federal and state standards.
SWAP014-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The installed equipment must be an ENERGY STAR - qualified heat pump clothes dryer that meets or exceeds Version 1.1 minimum efficiency CEF Specification. The product does not need to be qualified as ENERGY STAR Most Efficient.
Existing base equipment must be a stand-alone gas dryer.
SWAP014-04.Quality Assurance: Verify that the new unit is ENERGY STAR qualified.
SWAP014-04.Important Notes: This is a fuel substitution measure.

SWAP015-03.Offering ID: The measure case is either the replacement of fully electric range with an electric oven with induction cooktop or a standalone electric resistance cooktop with a standalone induction cooktop. Measure case specification was informed by the Codes and Standards Enhancement (CASE): Plug Loads and Lighting Modeling (Measure Number: 2016-RES-ACM-D) and the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy Office.
SWAP015-03.Base Case Description: An electric range with electric resistance cooktop or a standalone electric resistance cooktop. The base case specification was informed by the Codes and Standards Enhancement (CASE): Plug Loads and Lighting Modeling (Measure Number: 2016-RES-ACM-D).
SWAP015-03.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
Installed equipment must:
  • Be an electric range with induction cooktop or a standalone induction cooktop.
  • Have four or five burners.
Used or rebuilt equipment is not eligible.
SWAP015-03.Important Notes: For offerings with ranges, both measure and base case have electric resistance oven components; ovens efficiencies are equivalent and are not included in the savings calculations.

SWAP015-04.Offering ID: An ENERGY STAR qualified electric range with induction cooktop, electric range with electric resistance cooktop, induction cooktop, or electric resistance cooktop. Measure case specification is based on integrated annual energy consumption (IAEC) specified by ENERGY STAR® Program Requirements Product Specification for Residential Electric Cooking Products Version 1.0.
SWAP015-04.Base Case Description: A standard efficiency electric range or a standard efficiency standalone electric cooktop. Base case specifications were informed by the 2019 California Residential Appliance Saturation Study (RASS).
SWAP015-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
Installed equipment must:
  • Be ENERGY STAR-qualified
  • Have four or five burners.

Used or rebuilt equipment is not eligible.
SWAP015-04.Important Notes:
For offerings with ranges, both measure and base case have electric resistance oven components; ovens efficiencies are equivalent and are not included in the savings calculations.
The analysis references information derived from the 2019 California Residential Saturation Study (RASS), the Department of Energy (DOE) Energy Conservation Program: Energy Conservation Standards for Consumer Conventional Cooking Products proposed rule, U.S. Energy Information Administration's (EIA) Residential Energy Consumption Survey (RECS), and ENERGY STAR Residential Electric Cooking Products Specification.
HVAC interactive effects are excluded due to reduced kitchen hood consumption.

SWAP017-03.Offering ID: A residential gas oven model that performed highest in both preheat energy consumption and cooking energy efficiency, based on SoCalGas’s (SCG) Food Service Testing Lab and ovens tested in the 2019 Residential Oven Performance and Energy Comparison Study conducted for SCG by Frontier Energy.
SWAP017-03.Base Case Description: A residential gas oven that has lower cooking efficiency or higher preheat consumption than the measure case threshold.
SWAP017-03.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The measure is limited to gas ovens located underneath standalone ranges, slide-in gas ranges, and wall ovens.
All residential ovens must be tested by an ISO 17025-accredited laboratory using ASTM F1496.
The following are not eligible:
  • Ovens with a cavity capacity <3 cu.ft. for standalone range oven types
  • Gas ranges with electric ovens
  • Commercial ovens

SWAP017-04.Offering ID: A residential gas oven model that performed highest in both preheat energy consumption and cooking energy efficiency, based on SoCalGas’s (SCG) Food Service Testing Lab and ovens tested in the 2019 Residential Oven Performance and Energy Comparison Study conducted for SCG by Frontier Energy.
SWAP017-04.Base Case Description: A residential gas oven that has lower cooking efficiency or higher preheat consumption than the measure case threshold.
SWAP017-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The measure is limited to gas ovens located underneath standalone ranges, slide-in gas ranges, and wall ovens.
All residential ovens must be tested by an ISO 17025-accredited laboratory using ASTM F1496.
The following are not eligible:
  • Ovens with a cavity capacity <3 cu.ft. for standalone range oven types
  • Gas ranges with electric ovens
  • Commercial ovens

SWAP020-01.Offering ID: A portable air-conditioner (AC) or heat pump (HP) that exceeds the combined energy efficiency ratio (CEER) requirements of California Appliance Efficiency Regulations (Title 20) by ≥5% and uses a refrigerant, listed in the US Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) Significant New Alternatives Program (SNAP), with ≤1000 global warming potential (GWP). Some offerings require connected controls.
SWAP020-01.Base Case Description: A portable AC and HP that meets Title 20 minimum CEER for the seasonal adjusted cooling capacity (SACC).
SWAP020-01.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The following eligibility requirements apply:
  • For “Connected Controls” offerings, the product should have some ability to wirelessly connect to a smart home system (Alexa, Google Home, Apple Home, etc.), website, or mobile phone application. There are no specific requirements for demand response enabled controls.
  • The portable AC/HP must use an EPA SNAP listed refrigerant with a 100-year GWP ≤1000.
  • The portable AC/HP must be UL certified for relevant UL safety standards by a Nationally Recognized Testing Laboratory (NRTL).
  • The portable HP must not use electric resistance heat as the primary heating source.
  • Both single-duct and dual-duct products are eligible, as long as they meet all other product requirements.
SWAP020-01.Quality Assurance: The minimum qualifying measure case CEER varies by SACC and should be confirmed for each product. Alternatively, the qualifying for a specific product can be found by multiplying the value in the "Combined Energy Efficiency Ratio (CEER) in Btu/Wh Standard" column from the Title 20 appliance database qualifying product list by 105% and rounding to nearest tenth.
SWAP020-01.Important Notes: This is a POU-only measure; this measure package has not been reviewed and approved by the California Public Utilities Commission (CPUC) but has been affirmed by the California Technical Forum.

SWBE001-04.Offering ID: The installation of a single-layer heat curtain in a greenhouse area in which a heat curtain was not present or functional.
SWBE001-04.Base Case Description: Greenhouse area without any heat curtains or with nonfunctional heat curtains.
SWBE001-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
Eligible products must meet the following requirements:
  • Must be a single-layer interior curtain installed for heat retention.
  • Must be installed in a gas-heated greenhouse facility.
  • The facility must be a greenhouse with the primary purpose of agricultural use.
  • The heat curtain must be a new curtain installed where none previously existed or is no longer functional.
  • The heat curtain must be installed above the conditioned area where the gas heat source provides hot air to plant and seed species.
  • The heat curtain must have a natural gas savings rating that meets or exceeds 40%.
  • The heat curtain must have a warranty/product life of five years.
  • The installation must allow the curtain to be automatically or manually moved into place.

The square footage of the new heat curtain cannot exceed the square footage of the greenhouse floor. Any overhang and overlap of curtain material cannot be included in the square footage calculation.
SWBE001-04.Important Notes: The analysis assumed that the thermal curtains are deployed during nighttime hours, and open during daytime hours.

SWBE001-05.Offering ID: The installation of a single-layer heat curtain in a greenhouse area in which a heat curtain was not present or functional.
SWBE001-05.Base Case Description: Greenhouse area without any heat curtains or with nonfunctional heat curtains.
SWBE001-05.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
Eligible products must meet the following requirements:
  • Must be a single-layer interior curtain installed for heat retention.
  • Must be installed in a gas-heated greenhouse facility.
  • The facility must be a greenhouse with the primary purpose of agricultural use.
  • The heat curtain must be a new curtain installed where none previously existed or is no longer functional.
  • The heat curtain must be installed above the conditioned area where the gas heat source provides hot air to plant and seed species.
  • The heat curtain must have a natural gas savings rating that meets or exceeds 40%.
  • The heat curtain must have a warranty/product life of five years.
  • The installation must allow the curtain to be automatically or manually moved into place.
The square footage of the new heat curtain cannot exceed the square footage of the greenhouse floor. Any overhang and overlap of curtain material cannot be included in the square footage calculation.
SWBE001-05.Important Notes: The analysis assumed that the thermal curtains are deployed during nighttime hours, and open during daytime hours.

SWBE002-04.Offering ID: A greenhouse roof with infrared (IR) inhibiting film additive on the inflated double polyethylene roof
SWBE002-04.Base Case Description: A greenhouse with an inflated double polyethylene roof without an infrared (IR) film additive.
SWBE002-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The installation of infrared (IR) film must meet the following requirements:
  • The IR film must be installed in a gas-heated greenhouse facility.
  • The facility must be a greenhouse with the primary purpose of agricultural use.
  • The film must be infrared, anti-condensate, polyethylene plastic with a minimum thickness of 0.006in.
  • The IR film shall not be installed on the walls of the greenhouse.
SWBE002-04.Important Notes: Savings were adopted from DEER and vary by Building HVAC.

SWBE002-05.Offering ID: A greenhouse roof with infrared (IR) inhibiting film additive on the inflated double polyethylene roof
SWBE002-05.Base Case Description: A greenhouse with an inflated double polyethylene roof without an infrared (IR) film additive.
SWBE002-05.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The installation of infrared (IR) film must meet the following requirements:
  • The IR film must be installed in a gas-heated greenhouse facility.
  • The facility must be a greenhouse with the primary purpose of agricultural use.
  • The film must be infrared, anti-condensate, polyethylene plastic with a minimum thickness of 0.006in.
  • The IR film shall not be installed on the walls of the greenhouse.
SWBE002-05.Important Notes: Savings were adopted from DEER and vary by Building HVAC.

SWBE006-03.Offering ID: The measure case is the installation of ceiling insulation, defined by the R-value, on top of vintage specific ceiling insulation.
SWBE006-03.Base Case Description: The base case is defined as the customer’s existing insulation, specified in the Database for Energy Efficient Resources (DEER) model.
SWBE006-03.Important Notes: Savings were adopted from DEER and vary by Building HVAC.

SWBE006-04.Offering ID: The measure case is the installation of ceiling insulation, defined by the R-value, on top of vintage specific ceiling insulation.
SWBE006-04.Base Case Description: The base case is defined as the customer’s existing insulation, specified in the Database for Energy Efficient Resources (DEER) model.
SWBE006-04.Important Notes: Savings were adopted from DEER and vary by Building HVAC.

SWBE007-03.Offering ID: The measure case is the installation of wall insulation, defined by the R-value, into a wall with no existing insulation.
SWBE007-03.Base Case Description: The base case is defined as an existing 2x4 wall with no existing insulation.
SWBE007-03.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
This measure is applicable only in pre-1988 vintage in all residential single-family, multifamily and double wide mobile home dwelling units. Residential wall insulation became required by Title 24 new construction code in 1988.
Replacement of or addition to existing wall insulation is not eligible.
SWBE007-03.Quality Assurance:
Ensure that preexisting walls have no insulation.
Verify that the wall has conditioned space on one side and unconditioned space on the other side.
Verify the installation insulation R-value.
Verify that installation of insulation is for a building built before 1988.

SWBE007-04.Offering ID: The measure case is the installation of wall insulation, defined by the R-value, into a wall with no existing insulation.
SWBE007-04.Base Case Description: The base case is defined as an existing 2x4 wall with no existing insulation.
SWBE007-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
This measure is applicable only in pre-1988 vintage in all residential single-family, multifamily and double wide mobile home dwelling units. Residential wall insulation became required by Title 24 new construction code in 1988.
Replacement of or addition to existing wall insulation is not eligible.
SWBE007-04.Quality Assurance:
Ensure that preexisting walls have no insulation.
Verify that the wall has conditioned space on one side and unconditioned space on the other side.
Verify the installation insulation R-value.
Verify that installation of insulation is for a building built before 1988.

SWBE010-01.Offering ID:
The measure case is the combination of the following retrofits for an existing single-family home with central air conditioning and/or furnace with air distribution ducts located in the vented attic space:
  • Repair and seal the existing air distribution ducts, unhanging them from the rafters before sealing, shorten and straighten them where possible
  • Seal the attic plane to reduce the infiltration from the attic to the conditioned space below
  • Increase the overall level of attic insulation such that the air distribution ducts are deeply buried under loose-fill insulation to a level at least 4 inches above the top of the ducts without creating visible insulation mounds (i.e., achieve a uniform level of loose-fill insulation across the attic).
SWBE010-01.Base Case Description: The base case is defined as a single family home with a "high" or "medium" duct leakage rate, and the customer’s existing insulation, if any.
SWBE010-01.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
This measure does not consider either the conversion of a vented attic into a non-vented attic or moving the existing air distribution ducts into conditioned space.
Ducts must be located in a ventilated attic with leakage no greater than 12 percent of system flow. Single family dwellings without air distribution ducts located in a vented attic are excluded.
Duct Retrofit: All duct sealing materials and methods shall comply with Underwriters Laboratories, Inc. Standard for Air Ducts, UL181-1972.
Ceiling Plane Air Sealing: Preference shall be given to flexible materials such as mastics manufactured for the purpose of building enclosure sealing. Mastic materials exhibit greater durability than air sealing foam, as foam does not expand and contract with the building enclosure as readily as materials that maintain flexibility over time. Foam may be used for large gaps, backfill, or other circumstances deemed more practical than flexible materials.
Attic Insulation: All attic insulation materials shall comply with Title 24 Section 110.8(d) & Section 150.0(a).
To be consistent with Duct Sealing measure package, this measure is only applicable in all residential single family homes built in or prior to 2006 and have a duct system in a vented attic space capable of achieving a R-60 insulation level.

SWBE013-01.Offering ID: A new exterior door installed in a residential building that is Title 24 compliant and ENERGY STAR® Residential Windows, Doors, and Skylights Version 7.0 qualified (U-factor ≤ 0.17; air leakage ≤ 0.3 cfm/sq.ft.). This measure is only applicable to opaque swinging single doors, opening from a conditioned space in a dwelling unit out to an exterior space.
SWBE013-01.Base Case Description: An existing exterior swinging door with a representative U-factor of 1.45 Btu/(hr-sq.ft.-°F) in single family and mobile homes and 0.82 Btu/(hr-sq.ft.-°F) in multifamily buildings, which aligns with the thermal properties used in the DEER2024 residential prototypes.
SWBE013-01.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
Installed products must be ENERGY STAR Residential Windows, Doors, and Skylights Version 7.0 qualified doors under the Swinging Entry Door (Single) product category, per Measure Case Description.
Only fully opaque doors are eligible. Doors with any glazing are excluded.
Any existing swinging exterior door between a conditioned indoor space and an unconditioned exterior space is eligible. Garage door opening from conditioned indoor space to unconditioned indoor space is not eligible. Sliding doors are not eligible.
Since the replacement of the door requires sealing per Title 24, this measure cannot be combined with door sealing or door sweep measures (SWBE014) for additional claimed savings.
SWBE013-01.Quality Assurance: Verify that unit meets the eligibility requirement (characteristic and rating) in the Measure Case Description.

SWBE014-01.Offering ID: The installation of a door sweep and/or door sealing for exterior doors.
SWBE014-01.Base Case Description: An existing exterior door without a door sweep and/or door sealing in residential buildings.
SWBE014-01.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
Only door sweeps and door sealing added to existing swinging exterior doors are eligible. An exterior door is defined as the door between a conditioned indoor space and an exterior space.
  • Garage door opening from conditioned indoor space to unconditioned indoor space is not eligible.
  • Replacement of existing door sweep or door sealing is not eligible.
  • Sliding doors are not eligible.
This measure is not eligible when combined with “SWBE013 Efficient Doors, Residential” for the same door(s). Measure is not eligible if installed in conjunction with a new exterior door, as exterior door sealing is required by Title 24.
Door sealing must be applied to all three sides (top and two vertical sides) of the door to be eligible.
SWBE014-01.Important Notes:
Door sealing and door sweeping can be implemented separately or together, and the savings can be combined for the latter case.
The normalizing unit for this measure package is per door treated.
The following Building Type - Climate Zone - Building HVAC permutations are excluded due to having negative savings:
  • DMo - CZ06 - rDXHP
  • DMo - CZ07 - rDXHP
  • DMo - CZ09 - rDXHP

SWCA001-04.Offering ID: A variable speed drive (VSD) on an existing rotary screw air compressor with rated capacity ≥5 hp (4 kW) and <25 hp (18 kW). Due to California Building Energy Efficiency Requirements (Title 24), measure offerings are not specified for compressor air systems ≥25 hp.
SWCA001-04.Base Case Description: The base case is defined as an existing rotary screw compressor, with rated capacity ≥5 hp (4 kW) and <25 hp (18 kW), using load/unload controls. For systems with multiple compressors, the base case compressor operates as a trim compressor and the online capacity of the compressed air system must be <25 hp (18 kW).
SWCA001-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The existing compressor shall meet the following eligibility requirements:
  • Must be a rotary screw compressor
  • Must have a horsepower rating ≥5 hp and <25 hp (18 kW)
  • Must operate in a stand-alone capacity or as a trim compressor (i.e., not base loaded in a multiple compressor system)
  • Must be permanently installed
  • Customer compressed air pressure and flow rate must not be modified. Customer baseline hours of operation must remain the same
  • Customer must comply with Title 24: Section 120.6(e). Mandatory Requirements for Compressed Air Systems.

Portable compressors and cyclic refrigerated dryers are not eligible.
SWCA001-04.Quality Assurance: Ensure that the VSD is installed correctly.

SWCA001-05.Offering ID: A variable speed drive (VSD) on an existing rotary screw air compressor with rated capacity ≥5 hp (4 kW) and <25 hp (18 kW). Due to California Building Energy Efficiency Requirements (Title 24), measure offerings are not specified for compressor air systems ≥25 hp.
SWCA001-05.Base Case Description: The base case is defined as an existing rotary screw compressor, with rated capacity ≥5 hp (4 kW) and <25 hp (18 kW), using load/unload controls. For systems with multiple compressors, the base case compressor operates as a trim compressor and the online capacity of the compressed air system must be <25 hp (18 kW).
SWCA001-05.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The existing compressor shall meet the following eligibility requirements:
  • Must be a rotary screw compressor
  • Must have a horsepower rating ≥5 hp and <25 hp (18 kW)
  • Must operate in a stand-alone capacity or as a trim compressor (i.e., not base loaded in a multiple compressor system)
  • Must be permanently installed
  • Customer compressed air pressure and flow rate must not be modified. Customer baseline hours of operation must remain the same
  • Customer must comply with Title 24: Section 120.6(e). Mandatory Requirements for Compressed Air Systems.

Portable compressors and cyclic refrigerated dryers are not eligible.
SWCA001-05.Quality Assurance: Ensure that the VSD is installed correctly.

SWCR001-04.Offering ID: Anti-sweat heater (ASH) controls for a reach-in low-temperature (LT) display case (freezer, case temperature <32°F) or a medium-temperature (MT) display case (cooler, case temperature ≥32°F).
SWCR001-04.Base Case Description: A standard, fixed anti-sweat heater (ASH) for reach-in low-temperature or medium-temperature display case that operates at full power, i.e., 100% of the time.
SWCR001-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The display case must be equipped with humidity-sensing, dew point based, or moisture/conductivity sensing controls that reduce the amount of power supplied to the heaters as the store dew point (DP) temperature decreases. Both dew point and conductivity sensing controls have been shown to result in higher savings than humidity-sensing controls used to model the savings for this measure. Therefore, these controls are considered eligible because claimed savings should be conservative. Typically, reduction in power consumption lessens when relative humidity levels reach 55% and lower. When humidity sensor controls are implemented, power consumption should decrease by at least 2% for every percentage the humidity falls below 55%.
This measure is applicable for grocery, lodging (hotel), restaurant (fast-food), restaurant (sit-down), retail (multistory large), retail (single-story large), and retail (small) buildings since the use of ASH controls is most common in these building types.
To avoid double-counting savings, this measure cannot be used in conjunction with:
  • SWCR015 and/or SWCR002, which include the new refrigeration display case with doors offerings.
  • SWCR002, which includes the low/no anti-sweat heat on low-temperature display case offerings.
  • Existing medium temperature and low temperature refrigeration display cases without anti-sweat heaters in them.

SWCR001-05.Offering ID: Anti-sweat heater (ASH) controls for a reach-in low-temperature (LT) display case (freezer, case temperature <32°F) or a medium-temperature (MT) display case (cooler, case temperature ≥32°F).
SWCR001-05.Base Case Description: A standard, fixed anti-sweat heater (ASH) for reach-in low-temperature or medium-temperature display case that operates at full power, i.e., 100% of the time.
SWCR001-05.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The ASH controls must be installed on either an existing reach-in low-temperature or an existing medium-temperature display case that have an existing ASH operating at 100% power continuously. The existing ASH must not have any existing ASH controls, including non-operable or broken, installed that allow for varying ASH power.
The ASH control product must have humidity-sensing, dew point based, or moisture/conductivity sensing controls that reduce the amount of power supplied to the heaters as the store dew point (DP) temperature decreases. Both dew point and conductivity sensing controls have been shown to result in higher savings than humidity-sensing controls used to model the savings for this measure. Therefore, these controls are considered eligible because claimed savings are conservative.
This measure applies to buildings with single and multiplex compressor systems. The energy savings were modeled using multiplex refrigeration systems, which are more efficient than single compressor systems. Since single compressor systems use more energy, adding ASH controls would consequently save more energy, so it is assumed that the generated energy savings are conservatively applicable to display cases with single-compressor systems.
This measure is applicable for grocery, lodging (hotel), restaurant (fast-food), restaurant (sit-down), retail (multistory large), retail (single-story large), and retail (small) buildings since the use of ASH controls is most common in these building types.
Only buildings with electric cooling and natural gas heating HVAC systems are eligible.
To avoid double-counting savings, this measure cannot be used in conjunction with:
  • SWCR015 and/or SWCR002, which include the new refrigeration display case with doors offerings.
  • SWCR002, which includes the low/no anti-sweat heat on low-temperature display case offerings.
  • Existing medium temperature and low temperature refrigeration display cases without anti-sweat heaters in them.

SWCR002-04.Offering ID: The replacement of standard low-temperature (LT) (freezer, case temperature <32°F) display case doors with special display case glass doors that have no anti-sweat heaters (ASHs). The ASH are eliminated but the heater on the case frame and/or mullion of the cases continues to operate.
SWCR002-04.Base Case Description: The existing low-temperature, reach-in display case equipped with standard glass doors with anti-sweat heaters (ASH) and humidity-based ASH control.
SWCR002-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The installation must meet the following requirements:
  • An existing standard glass door of a low-temperature reach-in display case is replaced with a special glass door without ASH on the glass doors or frame. The display case and/or case frame heater may continue to operate.
  • The display case temperature setpoint is between 5°F and 24°F.
  • The replacement door will have some kind of high efficiency technology including, but not limited to, double-pane glass, triple-pane glass, high efficiency glass, heat-reflective treatment, gas-fill, condensation resistant coatings, or advanced gasket and rail designs that prevent condensation on the door, such that ASH is not required on the door itself.
  • The final total wattage from the mullion and case frame heaters does not exceed 7.1 W/sq.ft. of door opening.
  • The total final mullion and case frame heaters amperage (at 120 volts) cannot exceed 0.925 amps per door.

This measure is applicable for grocery, lodging (hotel), restaurant (fast-food), restaurant (sit-down), retail (multistory large), retail (single-story large), and retail (small) buildings, since the use of low-temperature display cases is most common in these building types.
To avoid double-counting savings, measure cannot be used in conjunction with SWCR001.
SWCR002-04.Quality Assurance: The width of the new door itself measured in linear feet should be used for the incentivized length.

SWCR002-05.Offering ID: The replacement of standard low-temperature (LT) (freezer, case temperature <32°F) display case doors with special display case glass doors that have no anti-sweat heaters (ASHs). The ASH are eliminated but the heater on the case frame and/or mullion of the cases continues to operate.
SWCR002-05.Base Case Description: The existing low-temperature, reach-in display case equipped with standard glass doors with anti-sweat heaters (ASH) and humidity-based ASH control.
SWCR002-05.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The installation must meet the following requirements:
  • The existing display case must be a freezer.The existing door must be a standard glass door of a low-temperature reach-in display case that has ASH. The energy savings were calculated assuming an existing system with ASH controls installed, and therefore can conservatively be applied to existing case systems that do not have ASH controls.
  • The new replacement door must not have ASH on the door itself (i.e. door glass or door frame). The case frame and/or mullion may continue to have heaters.
  • The replacement door must have some kind of high efficiency technology including, but not limited to, double-pane glass, triple-pane glass, high efficiency glass, heat-reflective treatment, gas-fill, condensation resistant coatings, or advanced gasket and rail designs that prevent condensation on the door, such that ASH is not required on the door itself.
  • Some efficient door products may come as a package of door and case frame which helps to facilitate sealing and insulation to further reduce infiltration and condensation when the door is closed. These types of door and case frame system products are eligible for this measure package as long as the other eligibility requirements described herein are met.
  • This measure applies to buildings with single and multiplex compressor systems. The energy savings were modeled using multiplex refrigeration systems, which are more efficient than single compressor systems. Since single compressor systems use more energy, adding ASH controls would consequently save more energy, so it is assumed that the generated energy savings are conservatively applicable to display cases with single-compressor systems.

This measure is applicable for grocery, lodging (hotel), restaurant (fast-food), restaurant (sit-down), retail (multistory large), retail (single-story large), and retail (small) buildings, since the use of low-temperature display cases is most common in these building types.
Only buildings with electric cooling and natural gas heating HVAC systems are eligible.
To avoid double-counting savings, measure cannot be used in conjunction with SWCR001.
SWCR002-05.Quality Assurance: The width of the new door itself measured in linear feet should be used for the incentivized length for claims.

SWCR003-03.Offering ID: A new high efficiency electronically commutated permanent magnet direct current (EC) or permanent magnet synchronous alternating current (PMS) motor/fan assembly for an evaporator in a low-temperature (freezer, case temperature <32°F) or a medium-temperature (cooler, case temperature ≥32°F) refrigerated display case. An assembly with one motor, fan blades, and housing are treated the same as a bare motor.
SWCR003-03.Base Case Description: The existing equipment may be an existing low efficiency shaded pole (SHP) or permanent split capacitor (PSC) evaporator fan motor. The industry standard practice equipment is defined as an equivalent size evaporator fan with an EC motor, determined on a survey findings reported in ET17SCE8020 study.
SWCR003-03.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The installation must meet the following requirements:
  • The motor is installed as an evaporator fan motor in a refrigerated display case.
  • The new motor is the available product most closely matched to the original motor's speed and shaft output power (i.e., “equivalent size”).
  • The refrigerated display case may be a low-temperature or a medium-temperature.
  • The motor must be described as a low-power or high-efficiency model by the manufacturer.

This measure is applicable in conditioned spaces.
To avoid double-counting savings, this measure cannot be used in conjunction with SWAP011, SWCR002, SWCR018, SWCR019, SWCR021, and SWCR020.
SWCR003-03.Quality Assurance: Verify the motor type and the refrigerated equipment.

SWCR003-04.Offering ID: A new high efficiency electronically commutated permanent magnet direct current (ECM) or permanent magnet synchronous alternating current (PMS) motor/fan assembly for an evaporator in a low-temperature (freezer, case temperature <32°F) or a medium-temperature (cooler, case temperature ≥32°F) refrigerated display case. An assembly with one motor, fan blades, and housing are treated the same as a bare motor.
SWCR003-04.Base Case Description: The existing equipment may be an existing low efficiency shaded pole (SHP) or permanent split capacitor (PSC) evaporator fan motor. The industry standard practice equipment is defined as an equivalent size evaporator fan with an EC motor, determined on a survey findings reported in ET17SCE8020 study.
SWCR003-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The installation must meet the following requirements:
  • The motor is installed as an evaporator fan motor in a refrigerated display case.
  • The new motor is the available product most closely matched to the original motor's speed and shaft output power (i.e., “equivalent size”).
  • The refrigerated display case may be a low-temperature or a medium-temperature.
  • The motor must be described as a low-power or high-efficiency model by the manufacturer.

This measure is applicable in conditioned spaces.
To avoid double-counting savings, this measure cannot be used in conjunction with SWAP011, SWCR002, SWCR018, SWCR019, SWCR021, and SWCR020.
SWCR003-04.Quality Assurance: Verify the motor type and the refrigerated equipment.

SWCR007-04.Offering ID: Installation of a floating head pressure control, which set a minimum saturated condensing temperature (SCT) that follows the ambient (dry-bulb, Tdb) temperature for an air-cooled condenser or the wet bulb (Twb) temperature for an evaporatively-cooled condenser at the specified temperature difference (TD), in a multiplex refrigeration system to lower the compressor discharge pressure when weather permitted, effectively reducing the compressor energy consumption. The condenser system also includes a back-flood control setpoint (BSC) of 68°F that controls the temperature at or above which liquid refrigerant is back-flooded to (or retained in) the condenser, which increases the condenser pressure and lowers the compressor discharge pressure.
SWCR007-04.Base Case Description: A vintage-dependent commercial air-cooled or evaporatively cooled multiplex refrigeration system with a fixed saturated condensing temperature setpoint that does not follow the ambient condition.
SWCR007-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
To reduce floating head pressure to lower SCT, the equipment must meet the following requirements:
  • The controls are added to float head pressure down to a lower pressure when conditions permit (i.e., changes control from fixed setpoint to floating setpoint).
  • The controls only apply to refrigeration systems having multiplex compressor systems with existing control of the SCT at a fixed setpoint.
  • The new SCT setpoint must follow the ambient temperature by controlling condenser fans with variable-speed drives or by staging condenser fans.
Products cannot be used in conjunction with measures that already incorporate floating head pressure controls.
In addition, the following installations are ineligible:
  • Projects that only reprogram a controller; new hardware must be installed.
  • New construction installations.
  • Any improvements that will increase system energy use.
  • Building vintages and refrigeration multiplex system vintages after 2013 because the California Building Energy Efficiency Standards (Title 24) has mandated the floating controls since July 1, 2014.
  • Sub-cooler loads and air conditioning loads since the calculation of the design cooling load (tons) is based on connected display cases, walk-in coolers and freezers, cooled storage, and prep areas only.
SWCR007-04.Quality Assurance: Verify the vintage of the building and the system. The offerings are ineligible after July 1, 2014, due to Title 24 code mandates.

SWCR008-04.Offering ID: Installation of a floating suction pressure control that increases the suction temperature setpoint, which increases the compressor suction pressure and compressor efficiency, during periods of low loads based on the worst-case zone demand in a multiplex refrigeration system.
SWCR008-04.Base Case Description: A multiplex refrigeration system with an air-cooled condenser or an evaporative-cooled condenser with a fixed evaporator temperature setpoint.
SWCR008-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The equipment must meet the following requirements:
  • Convert the suction pressure controls of an existing multiplex system from fixed control to floating control.
  • Adjust suction pressure to the highest point that will still maintain setpoint temperatures at monitored cases on the suction circuit.
  • The controls must operate the refrigeration system according to worst-case demand, and the maximum suction setpoint is 5°F above the design temperature.
  • Customer must receive training materials including instruction to not override the suction pressure reset or have a service contract for the refrigeration system that includes maintaining controls settings.
The following installations are not eligible:
  • Projects that only reprogram a controller; new hardware must be installed.
  • New construction installations.
  • Floating suction pressure controls on refrigeration systems with variable speed evaporator fans.
  • Any improvements that will increase system energy use, such as new refrigeration loads, capacity expansion, condenser replacement, etc.
  • Building vintage/ system vintage after 2013 because the California Building Energy Efficiency Standards (Title 24) has mandated the floating controls since July 1, 2014.
  • Refrigeration systems with heat recovery for process or comfort heating.
  • Sub-cooler loads and air conditioning loads since the calculation of the design cooling load (tons) is based on connected display cases, walk-in coolers and freezers, cooled storage, and prep areas only.
SWCR008-04.Quality Assurance: Verify the vintage of the building and the system. The offerings are ineligible after July 1, 2014, due to Title 24 code mandates.

SWCR008-05.Offering ID: Installation of a floating suction pressure control that increases the suction temperature setpoint, which increases the compressor suction pressure and compressor efficiency, during periods of low loads based on the worst-case zone demand in a multiplex refrigeration system.
SWCR008-05.Base Case Description: A multiplex refrigeration system with an air-cooled condenser or an evaporative-cooled condenser with a fixed evaporator temperature setpoint.
SWCR008-05.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The equipment must meet the following requirements:
  • Convert the suction pressure controls of an existing multiplex system from fixed control to floating control. The product specifications must describe it as a floating suction controller by name or definition (adjust (or float) the temperature setpoint based on the load of the system)
  • Adjust suction pressure to the highest point that will still maintain setpoint temperatures at monitored cases on the suction circuit.
  • Customer should be provided with formal training and instructional materials by the manufacturer or installer of the floating suction control system. This training should cover the correct operation of the new equipment and explicitly instruct against overriding the suction temperature reset. Alternatively, customers may opt for a service contract for the refrigeration system, which would encompass the appropriate upkeep of the floating suction control system and its configurations.
The following installations are not eligible:
  • Projects that only reprogram a controller; new hardware installations are required, such as electronic suction regulators, case temperature sensors, controllers, and additional components. Floating suction pressure controls on refrigeration systems with variable speed evaporator fans.
  • Measure cannot be installed in conjunction with any improvements that will increase system energy use, such as new refrigeration loads, capacity expansion, condenser replacement, etc.
  • Refrigeration systems with heat recovery for process or comfort heating.
  • Replacement of broken/non-operational floating suction controls
  • Building vintage/ system vintage after 2013 because the California Building Energy Efficiency Standards (Title 24) has mandated the floating controls since July 1, 2014.
  • Sub-cooler loads and air conditioning loads since the calculation of the design cooling load (tons) is based on connected display cases, walk-in coolers and freezers, cooled storage, and prep areas only.
SWCR008-05.Quality Assurance: Verify the vintage of the building and the system. The offerings are ineligible after July 1, 2014, due to Title-24 code mandates.

SWCR010-04.Offering ID: The installation of insulation on un-insulated suction lines of medium-temperature (cooler, case temperature ≥32°F) and low-temperature (freezer, case temperature <32°F) refrigeration system.
SWCR010-04.Base Case Description: A refrigeration system with un-insulated suction refrigeration lines outside of the refrigerated space.
SWCR010-04.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The eligibility requirements are as follows:
  • The refrigeration suction line on existing equipment must be no more than 1-5/8 inches in diameter.
  • The existing refrigerant suction line should not be currently insulated nor have deteriorated insulation.
  • This measure is applicable to bare suction lines insulated with closed-cell nitrite rubber or equivalent with at least ¾-inch for medium-temperature and 1-inch for low-temperature systems.
  • Insulation R-values must be greater than or equal to R-3.2 for medium-temperature cooler lines and R-4.3 for low-temperature freezer.
  • The existing refrigerant suction line should not be currently insulated nor have deteriorated insulation.

SWCR010-05.Offering ID: The installation of insulation on un-insulated suction lines of medium-temperature (cooler, case temperature ≥32°F) and low-temperature (freezer, case temperature <32°F) refrigeration system.
SWCR010-05.Base Case Description: A refrigeration system with un-insulated suction refrigeration lines outside of the refrigerated space.
SWCR010-05.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The eligibility requirements are as follows:
  • Insulation must be of closed-cell elastomeric rubber at least ¾-inch thick for medium-temperature and 1-inch thick for low-temperature systems.
  • Insulation R-values must be greater than or equal to R-3.2 for medium-temperature cooler lines and R-4.3 for low-temperature freezer.
  • Insulation must have a flame spread index (FSI) rating of 25 or less and a smoke development index (SDI) rating of 50 or less (i.e. "25/50" rated) as tested to ASTM E84 standards.
  • The refrigeration suction line on existing equipment must be no more than 1-5/8 inches in diameter.
  • The existing refrigerant suction line should not be currently insulated nor have deteriorated insulation.

SWCR012-03.Offering ID: A Title 24-compliant, multiplex compressor refrigeration system, including both low- (freezer, case temperature <32°F) and medium-temperature (cooler, case temperature ≥32°F) sub-cooling.
SWCR012-03.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The eligibility requirements include the following:
  • Must replace stand-alone single-compressor system with a high-efficiency multiplex compressor system.
  • Must include floating head pressure control to a minimum 70°F setpoint.
  • Air-cooled condensers must use fan cycling or variable speed fans.

Floating head pressure control is not eligible.

SWCR015-03.Offering ID:
The retrofit of glass doors on a medium-temperature (cooler, case temperature ≥32°F), open-vertical, refrigerated display case (also known as an open multi-deck case).
Note:
  • “w/ night covers” means the base case includes night covers. Measure case does not require night covers.
  • “with LED” means the existing fluorescent lighting will be removed and the new display case doors will include LED lighting.
  • “without LED” means the existing display case will keep the LED lighting and the display case will not include new lighting.
SWCR015-03.Base Case Description: A medium-temperature, open-vertical, refrigerated display case with night covers on for six hours per night.
SWCR015-03.Other: Program Exclusion / /:
The measure may require changes to the refrigeration system serving the affected display case(s). These changes may include but are not limited to: replacing the expansion valve and/or evaporator pressure regulating valve, adjusting the evaporator temperature/pressure set point, resizing refrigeration piping, replacing the flood back valve on the condenser, resizing the coil/piping on applicable heat reclaim systems, and replacing or removing compressors. These potential changes stem from the significant reduction in the overall refrigeration load. Due to the complexity in determining which system alterations will be required at a site to maintain optimum system performance, a refrigeration contractor with design experience should be consulted before proceeding with the retrofit.
The following changes are not eligible:
  • The complete removal of the existing display case and replacing it with a new case with glass doors.
  • Total lighting power in the measure case may not exceed total lighting power in the base case. (Several manufacturers interviewed stated that most retrofits include a switch to more efficient LED case lighting, but not an overall increase in lighting power density.)
  • Anti-sweat heat (ASH) may not be present in the glass doors or door mounting. (Two out of three manufacturers stated that their products did not contain ASH in the door or mullion. Thus, it was decided to specifically prohibit door and frame heat, as it does not appear necessary in medium-temperature applications.)
SWCR015-03.Quality Assurance: Verify units meet the eligibility requirement in the Offering ID table.
SWCR015-03.Important Notes:
The following specifications apply to the base case analysis:
  • The supply air temperature is 32°F
  • The coil capacity is 1,675 Btu/hr-ft of case
  • Offering B base case has two rows of T8 light fixtures in the canopy (18.3 W/ft of case) and 13.3 W/ft of evaporator coil fan electric use
  • Offering A base case has LED lighting
  • The case uses off-cycle defrost, with four defrost cycles per day
